//: Template for `appshot compose`. Copy next to your captures and edit.
//output: Must be one of the store's accepted sizes; the compositor refuses anything else. This is the MAC shape. For iOS set "platform": "ios" and drop this key — each entry in "devices" carries its own canvas, and its id becomes a directory under source/ golden/ appstore/. See references/ios.md.
//fontFamily: The first family MUST be installed or captions silently fall back. 'SF Pro Display' is NOT part of a stock macOS install — it ships in Apple's SF font pack. appshot refuses to compose if it does not resolve; check with `appshot doctor`.
fontFamily: 'SF Pro Display', -apple-system, 'Helvetica Neue', Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif
//screens: Order here IS the store order — it stamps the 01-, 02-… prefix onto the output. Keep the capture filenames unnumbered so reordering the listing never means renaming images. `id` matches <id>~<appearance>.png in the --in dir.
//website: Optional. The basename `appshot compose website` emits into the marketing site, which takes the bare app UI. A screen with no `website` key is store-only — that is how you keep the paywall off your own pricing page. The site's order lives in its own component array, not here, so these files get no numeric prefix.
